analysis.device: NVIDIA RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell Server Edition
analysis.dtype: fp32 (cuDNN convs internally cast to TF32/FP16 tiles via convertTensor; no explicit AMP in graph)

bottleneck.description: Each Conv2d is followed by a BatchNorm2d that, because the model is in eval() mode (frozen running_mean/running_var), is a pure per-channel affine transform. Inductor lowers it to a chain of standalone memory-bound Triton kernels (triton_poi_fused__native_batch_3/4/5/6/7, triton_red_fused__native_batch_8, triton_per_fused__native_batch_9) that run AFTER the cuDNN conv kernel instead of being folded into it. These kernels do no useful FLOPs: they re-read the full activation tensor from DRAM, scale/shift it, and write it back. Counters confirm pure memory-bound waste: tensor_core_active_pct=0, and the largest BN kernel (triton_poi_fused__native_batch_5, grid 16384) sustains dram__throughput up to 73.5% (k_00063) and sm__warps_active 83-88% while doing only elementwise work. The conv sets bias=False, so the fold is exact (scale into weight, shift into a new bias).

evidence.kernel: triton_poi_fused__native_batch_5/6/7 + triton_red_fused__native_batch_8 + triton_per_fused__native_batch_9 (BN epilogue chain, repeated per conv stage)

transformation.description: Conv-BatchNorm folding for inference. Walk the Aten graph for the pattern aten::convolution.default / aten::cudnn_convolution.default feeding aten::_native_batch_norm_legit_no_training.default (the eval-mode BN op emitted by torch 2.11). Fold gamma/sqrt(var+eps) into the conv weight and (-mean*gamma/sqrt(var+eps)+beta) into a synthesized conv bias, then delete the BN node and rewire its users to the conv output. This collapses the 7-kernel BN epilogue per stage into zero extra kernels; the affine is absorbed into the conv weights at trace time.
transformation.location: Inductor post_grad_custom_pre_pass (torch 2.11 Aten-IR injection point; aot_autograd fw_compiler is broken on 2.11 per project memory)
fx_steps: weight, bias, running_mean, running_var = node.args[1], node.args[2], node.args[3], node.args[4]; eps = node.args[6]
# compute folded conv weight/bias from frozen BN params (const-folded on the parameter tensors)
folded_w, folded_b = fuse_conv_bn_weights(conv_weight, conv_bias_or_none, running_mean, running_var, eps, bn_gamma, bn_beta)
with gm.graph.inserting_before(conv): new_w = gm.graph.get_attr(folded_w_name); new_b = gm.graph.get_attr(folded_b_name)
transformation.code_hint: Reuse torch.nn.utils.fusion.fuse_conv_bn_weights for the numerics; in eval() torch traces aten._native_batch_norm_legit_no_training, so match that exact target (NOT aten.native_batch_norm) at the Aten IR level.

estimated_impact.memory_traffic_reduction: Eliminates one full activation read+write per BN kernel per stage. Largest stage activation (64->128 @ 64x64, 16384-block BN kernel) avoids a ~73%-DRAM-throughput pass each iteration; cumulatively removes the dominant memory-bound elementwise traffic from the conv epilogues.

optimizations.notes: Exact and numerically safe because conv bias=False and model is in eval() (frozen stats). Highest-value FX transform for this workload. Registers a new folded weight/bias constant; per the standing dtype-promotion rule this would need to run after any dtype promotion pass, but none is proposed here so there is no ordering conflict. duration_ns figures are ncu-inflated (2-5x); the 122us is a relative within-profile estimate, not absolute latency.

bottleneck.description: cuDNN's tensor-core conv path (sm80_xmma_fprop_implicit_gemm and the winograd 'Kernel') requires NHWC (channels_last) tensors, but the Inductor graph keeps activations in NCHW (contiguous). cuDNN therefore wraps every conv with convertTensor_kernel layout-shuffle kernels (NCHW->NHWC on input/weight before, and back after). These are pure overhead: zero tensor-core activity, near-zero L1/L2 hit rate (l1tex__t_sector_hit_rate=0, lts__t_request_hit_rate as low as 0.14%), and they run as streaming DRAM copies hitting up to 72.8% dram__throughput (k_00082). Every conv op carries 2-4 of these conversions; for op_id=12 the convertTensor kernels (k_00082 8352ns + k_00083 3264ns) are ~11% of the op's time, and they recur for all six conv stages.

evidence.kernel: convertTensor_kernel (NCHW<->NHWC layout shuffle wrapping each cuDNN conv)

transformation.description: Propagate channels_last (NHWC) memory format through the conv stack so cuDNN consumes tensors natively and drops the convertTensor wrappers. At the module level this is model.to(memory_format=torch.channels_last) + input.to(memory_format=torch.channels_last). At the Aten-IR level the pass annotates conv input/weight placeholders and intermediate aten::convolution.default outputs as channels_last and removes redundant aten::clone.default / contiguous-inducing nodes that force NCHW, letting Inductor lay out activation buffers as NHWC end-to-end. This collapses the back-to-back convertTensor pairs to at most a single conversion at the graph boundary.
transformation.location: Inductor post_grad_custom_pre_pass, applied after OPT-1 so the folded conv weights are the nodes whose layout is set
fx_steps: # module-level entry point (preferred, drives the Inductor layout planner):
# Aten-IR reinforcement pass to strip residual NCHW-forcing nodes around convs:
if isinstance(inp, torch.fx.Node) and inp.target is torch.ops.aten.clone.default and inp.kwargs.get('memory_format', None) is torch.contiguous_format:
transformation.code_hint: Validate that aten.convolution.default outputs carry channels_last strides after the pass; the early 3->64 conv (3 input channels) may still keep one conversion since NHWC offers little benefit at C=3.

estimated_impact.memory_traffic_reduction: Removes the redundant NCHW<->NHWC streaming copies (each a full-tensor DRAM read+write at up to ~73% peak throughput) around the 64->128 and 128->256 convs. The largest stages save one ~8.4us 73%-DRAM conversion per conv per iteration.

optimizations.notes: Medium (not high) because this is a layout policy that depends on Inductor's layout planner honoring the annotation rather than a single deterministic node rewrite, and because the 3-channel first conv will retain a conversion. Must be applied after OPT-1 so layout is set on the post-fold conv weights. No MCP tools were available to confirm the torch 2.11 layout-pass API, so this novel layout transform is capped at medium.

bottleneck.description: The first conv (3 input channels -> 64) is routed to sm80_xmma_fprop_implicit_gemm (k_00079/k_00098) and is memory/launch bound rather than compute bound: the op aggregate shows tensor_core_active_pct=0.0, sm_throughput=21.5%, eligible_cycles_pct=23.2 (<<100; on Blackwell warp_cycles_per_instruction is unavailable so eligible_cycles is the latency indicator). With only 3 input channels the GEMM contraction dimension cannot fill tensor-core tiles, so cuDNN falls back to a small implicit-GEMM kernel; the op is dominated by surrounding convertTensor copies (k_00077 789.5MB-class read at 8.7% dram, k_00078) feeding a tiny 10.8us GEMM. grid [2,512] over ~188 SMs is light per wave.

evidence.kernel: sm80_xmma_fprop_implicit_gemm (3->64 first conv) + its convertTensor feeders

transformation.description: This op is small and tensor-core-ineligible by construction (C_in=3). The most effective lever is folding its convertTensor feeders away via OPT-2 (channels_last), which removes the layout-conversion overhead that dominates this op. No standalone weight transform meaningfully engages tensor cores at C_in=3, so the recommendation is to let OPT-1+OPT-2 subsume it. If desired, an aten-level pad of the input channel dim (3->4) could be inserted before aten::convolution.default with a zero-padded weight, but the gain is marginal versus the added pad kernel.
transformation.location: subsumed by OPT-2 (channels_last); optional aten::constant_pad_nd.default insertion before the first aten::convolution.default
fx_steps: # Optional, low-value: pad C_in 3->4 to align tensor-core tiles
# locate first conv: node.target is torch.ops.aten.convolution.default with weight.shape[1]==3
# insert aten.constant_pad_nd on the channel dim of input and zero-pad weight[:, :1] accordingly
# NOT recommended unless OPT-1/OPT-2 leave this op as a measured hotspot
transformation.code_hint: Treat as covered by OPT-2; do not add a separate pass first. Re-profile after OPT-1/OPT-2 before considering channel padding.

estimated_impact.memory_traffic_reduction: Indirect: the convertTensor feeders (the bulk of this op) are removed by OPT-2. The residual implicit-GEMM kernel is left as-is.

optimizations.notes: Low confidence and low priority: a 3-channel input conv is fundamentally tensor-core-ineligible and tiny. Documented for completeness; the real fix is the layout cleanup in OPT-2. Channel padding is explicitly NOT recommended as a first move.

global_notes: Device NVIDIA RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell Server Edition (GB202, ~188 SMs). Blackwell removes warp_cycles_per_instruction (null throughout profile); eligible_cycles_pct (<20 == latency-bound) used as the latency indicator instead. No knowledge/hardware-limits.md present in repo, so SM count and ridge point are from built-in Blackwell GB202 knowledge.

4 operators below 1% threshold omitted (combined 1.49% of attributed time): aten::addmm_0, aten::convolution_0, aten::addmm(op_id=21), aten::addmm(op_id=42) -- the linear classifier head (gemmSN_TN, [16,256]x[256,10]) is negligible.

edge_case_flag fused_kernel_double_count set: six operators carry is_fused==true (aten::cudnn_convolution op_id=7/12/18/28/33/39, each fused_with the corresponding aten::_convolution and aten::convolution wrappers). Their aggregated durations therefore count the same GPU kernels under three op names; impact estimates use the cuDNN-level kernels directly to avoid triple-counting.

DOUBLE-ATTRIBUTION CAVEAT: the two largest budget entries -- layer::unique::prologue (37.5%) and aten::cudnn_convolution_0 (26.4%, no op_id) -- are iteration/NVTX roll-up ranges whose member kernels (k_00018..k_00074) overlap the per-op_id operators (op_id 7-42). The same physical kernels appear under multiple operators, so total_attributed_ns=1,229,726ns over-counts true wall time. Optimization reasoning is anchored on the cleaner per-op_id operators while citing the high-resolution hardware counters found in the prologue/cudnn_convolution_0 kernel lists.

All kernels run on stream_id=7 only -> no multi_stream_overlap. compile_mode=inductor (not cudagraphs) -> no cuda_graph_replay. start_ns ~4.7e9 (positive, <1e15) -> no clock_domain issue. 14 unattributed kernels / ~95 measured kernels = 14.7% -> below high_unattributed threshold; note all unattributed kernels are BatchNorm Triton kernels (triton_*_fused__native_batch_3..9) that OPT-1 eliminates anyway.

duration_ns values are ncu counter-collection inflated (2-5x); all relative_duration_reduction_ns figures are within-profile relative estimates derived from those durations, not absolute latencies. Re-profile after applying OPT-1 then OPT-2 to confirm.

Dependency DAG: OPT-1 (conv-bn fold) -> OPT-2 (channels_last on folded weights) -> OPT-3 (subsumed by OPT-2). No cycles. Inject passes via Inductor post_grad_custom_pre_pass on torch 2.11 (aot_autograd fw_compiler is broken on 2.11 per project memory).

MCP tools (context7, exa-search, memory, sequential-thinking) were not available; FX API patterns are from built-in knowledge and novel transforms (OPT-2 layout, OPT-3 padding) are capped at medium/low confidence accordingly.
